title: Stop applying AI to the old ways of working!
summary: Andrew Macvean from Google for Developers argues that organizations should not simply apply AI to traditional working methods. He contends that coding was never the primary bottleneck and emphasizes the need to redesign entire development lifecycles to be AI-native rather than adapting AI to legacy processes.
writeup: Andrew Macvean of Google for Developers argues that adding AI to existing development workflows misses the point, because coding was never the real bottleneck. The talk reframes the opportunity around redesigning entire development lifecycles and processes to be built with and for AI from the ground up. The core takeaway: gains come from rethinking workflows rather than automating the old ones, drawing on AI-assisted development, workflow automation, and pattern thinking.
